steps.title: Factor and match coefficients
steps.content:
If $px + q$ is a factor, the quadratic must factor as:
$$ax^2 + 130x + c = (px + q)(rx + s)$$
Expanding: $(px + q)(rx + s) = prx^2 + (ps + qr)x + qs$

Matching coefficients:
  • $x^2$: $a = pr$
  • $x$: $ps + qr = 130$
  • constant: $c = qs$

Since $a = pr$ and $c = qs$:
$$ac = (pr)(qs) = (ps)(qr)$$
To maximize $ac$, we need to maximize $(ps)(qr)$ subject to $ps + qr = 130$.

steps.title: Apply AM-GM to maximize the product
steps.content:
For a fixed sum, a product is maximized when the factors are equal. By the AM-GM inequality, with $ps + qr = 130$:
$$\sqrt{(ps)(qr)} \leq \frac{ps + qr}{2} = 65$$
So $(ps)(qr) \leq 65^2 = 4225$. Equality holds when $ps = qr = 65$, giving the greatest possible value of $ac$ as 4225.
